senses.definition: obstinate refusal to submit to established authority or to comply with a regulation
someone who is reluctant to submit to an authority
to control or adjust something in a way that agrees with rules and regulations

examples.x: The government agency works to regulate environmental policies to protect natural resources.
Businesses must adhere to laws that regulate fair competition in the marketplace.
Financial institutions are closely regulated to ensure stability and prevent fraud.
She frequently regulates the organization's procedures for efficiency.
The manager is actively regulating safety protocols for the workplace.

senses.definition: a rule made by the government, an authority, etc. to control or govern something within a particular area

examples.x: The new regulations require all vehicles to undergo emissions testing annually.
Financial regulations aim to ensure fair and transparent practices in the banking industry.
Building regulations dictate the minimum safety standards for construction projects.
The airline industry is subject to strict regulations regarding passenger safety and security.
Environmental regulations limit the amount of pollutants that factories can release into the air and water.

senses.definition: in accordance with the established rules, customs, etc.
to make something mandatory or necessary

examples.x: The event requires a ticket for entry.
The situation requires quick thinking and action.
The test requires a minimum score to pass.
The plan requires approval from the board before proceeding.
The event requires registration in advance.

senses.definition: something that is really needed or wanted

examples.x: The job listing specified that having a valid driver's license was a requirement for the position.
The application process had a requirement for submitting three professional references.
Meeting the age requirement is necessary for participation in the youth sports league.
Proof of citizenship is a requirement for obtaining a passport or visa.
Completing a health and safety training course is a requirement for working in certain industrial jobs.

senses.definition: to bring someone or something under control through laws and rules

examples.x: The school decided to restrict access to certain areas for student safety.
The government chose to restrict the use of specific chemicals due to environmental concerns.
To maintain order, the organization implemented rules to restrict behavior during events.
The city council voted to restrict parking in certain areas to ease traffic congestion.

senses.definition: limited or controlled by regulations or specific conditions
a rule or law that limits what one can do or the thing that can happen

examples.x: The new policy included a restriction on the amount of vacation time employees could take in a year.
The government imposed a restriction on the sale of certain chemicals to ensure public safety.
Dietary restrictions, such as avoiding gluten, were noted on the menu to accommodate guests with allergies.
The school's restriction on the use of mobile phones during class hours aimed to minimize distractions.
The rental agreement included a restriction on subletting the apartment without the landlord’s approval.

senses.definition: (of a rule, process, etc.) strictly followed or applied

examples.x: The school has a rigorous dress code that all students must follow.
To maintain safety, the laboratory follows rigorous procedures for handling chemicals.
The university's rigorous admission process ensures that only the most qualified students are accepted.
She underwent rigorous training to prepare for the marathon.
The company follows a rigorous quality control process to ensure product reliability.
His rigorous approach to research yielded groundbreaking results in the field of medicine.
His training was rigorous, pushing him to exceed his limits.

senses.definition: in a thorough or demanding manner

examples.x: The researchers rigorously analyzed the data to ensure the validity of their scientific findings.
The fitness trainer designed a rigorously structured workout plan to maximize the clients' physical endurance.
She studied for the exam rigorously, covering every topic in detail.
The quality control team rigorously inspected each product to maintain high standards of production.
The project manager implemented a rigorously organized schedule to meet tight deadlines.

senses.definition: an instruction that says what is or is not allowed in a given situation or while playing a game

examples.x: The first rule of the game is to always play fair.
He broke the rule by bringing his phone into the examination room.
The school has a strict dress code rule that all students must follow.
According to the rule, players must stay within the boundaries of the field.
The new rule requires everyone to wear masks in public spaces.

senses.definition: a set of rules and regulations that must be followed in a particular organization, occupation, etc.

examples.x: Before starting the tournament, each participant received a copy of the rule book outlining the competition's regulations.
The student athletes must familiarize themselves with the school's sports rule book to understand expectations and guidelines.
The employees were required to review the company's rule book regarding workplace safety before beginning their new jobs.
The chess club members referred to the rule book to resolve disputes and clarify tournament procedures.
The referee consulted the league's rule book to determine whether the player's conduct warranted a penalty.

senses.definition: to command someone to do something immediately, often as punishment

examples.x: The teacher slapped on detention for the student who was caught cheating on the exam.
The coach slapped on extra laps for the team members who arrived late to practice.
The manager slapped on a fine for employees who repeatedly violated company policies.
The principal slapped on a suspension for the students involved in the cafeteria food fight.
The officer slapped on a ticket for drivers who parked illegally in the restricted zone.

senses.definition: an officially written and established law

examples.x: The new statute requires all drivers to use hands-free devices while driving.
She studied the statute carefully to understand her legal obligations.
He challenged the statute in court, arguing that it was unconstitutional.
A recent statute mandates stricter environmental regulations for industrial facilities.
Under the statute, the company must provide annual safety training for employees.

senses.definition: according to or allowed by law

examples.x: The company's actions were found to be in compliance with statutory regulations.
Statutory holidays are days designated by law where businesses and institutions are typically closed.
Employers must adhere to statutory requirements regarding minimum wage and working hours.
The city council passed a statutory ordinance prohibiting outdoor smoking in public parks.
Tax deductions are subject to statutory limits set forth in the Internal Revenue Code.

senses.definition: (in the UK) a type of order that has legal status and is issued by a government minister or someone with a legal authority
to specify that something needs to be done or how it should be done, especially as part of an agreement

examples.x: The contract stipulates that the payment must be made within 30 days of the invoice date.
The rental agreement stipulates that no pets are allowed in the apartment.
The employment contract stipulates the number of hours the employee is expected to work each week.
The trade agreement stipulates the terms and conditions for the exchange of goods between the two countries.
Before signing the lease, it's crucial to carefully read and understand the terms stipulated by the landlord.

senses.definition: an official statement, condition, or agreement to do or forbear something
(of rules and regulations) absolute and must be obeyed under any circumstances

examples.x: The company has strict guidelines in place to ensure workplace safety for all employees.
The school enforces strict dress code regulations to maintain a professional learning environment.
The immigration laws in this country are very strict, making it difficult for some to obtain visas.
The library has a strict policy against overdue books, imposing fines for late returns.

senses.definition: the quality or characteristic of being uncompromising in enforcing rules, regulations, or standards

examples.x: The teacher's strictness ensured that all students followed classroom rules.
The company's policy on punctuality reflects its strictness regarding employee attendance.
Military training is known for its strictness in discipline and physical conditioning.
Parental strictness varied among households, influencing children's behavior and development.
Some admired his strictness, while others found it intimidating.

senses.definition: in a way that demands or requires complete obedience

examples.x: Employees are strictly prohibited from using personal devices during work hours.
The school enforces a strictly enforced dress code to maintain a professional environment.
The security guards are instructed to strictly monitor access to restricted areas.
The contract terms must be strictly adhered to in order to avoid legal consequences.

senses.definition: (of a law, regulation, rule, etc.) extremely limiting and strict

examples.x: The company had stringent rules about employee conduct.
The government imposed stringent measures to control the spread of the virus.
They had to comply with stringent safety standards on the construction site.
The university’s stringent admission requirements made it difficult to get in.
The environmental group pushed for more stringent laws to protect endangered species.

senses.definition: the state or act of accepting defeat and not having a choice but to obey the person in the position of power

examples.x: The rebels were forced into submission after their stronghold was captured by government forces.
His quiet submission to the harsh demands of his boss was noticed by his sympathetic colleagues.
The conquered nation was brought to submission, agreeing to all the terms imposed by the victors.
The athlete's submission in the final round of the match surprised many of his fans.
Her submission to the authority of the ruling party was evident in her compliance with their policies.

senses.definition: to be required to do something, especially because of a rule, agreement, tradition, etc.

examples.x: He was supposed to finish the report by Friday, as per the company's guidelines.
We are supposed to wear formal attire for the event tonight.
The students were supposed to hand in their assignments before the deadline.
They are supposed to attend the weekly meetings as part of their responsibilities.
He was supposed to call her once he arrived at the airport.

senses.definition: the exact literal interpretation of a law or agreement as opposed to its general meaning

examples.x: The agent counsels them obliquely against waste, repeats the letter of the law, and smiles.
The heart of all this is going beyond the letter of the law with your clients.

senses.definition: ‌(in the UK) a written instruction given to MPs by the leader of their party that states they should vote in a particular way on a specific issue in parliament
uncompromising in one's expectations, rules, or approach to dealing with others

examples.x: His tough management style earned him a reputation for achieving results, albeit with high expectations.
The sergeant was known for being tough but fair, instilling discipline and accountability in the troops.
In negotiations, his tough stance on pricing secured a favorable deal for the company.
The judge's tough sentencing reflected the seriousness of the crime and deterred future offenders.

senses.definition: to make something such as a rule or policy stronger
in every instance, without exception

examples.x: She arrives at work at 8 a.m. without fail every single day.
I send a thank-you note without fail after every interview.
He calls his parents every Sunday without fail.
The mail arrives at my house by noon without fail.
The team practices every morning without fail, no matter the weather.
